channel.title: NDTV News Search Records Found 1000
channel.description: NDTV.com provides the latest information from and in-depth coverage of India and the world. Find breaking news, India news, top stories, elections, politics, business, cricket, movies, lifestyle, health, live TV, videos, photos and more.

item.title: US-Iran Peace Deal Could Save India Billions On Oil Import Bill

media:content.media:description: Brent crude prices drop after US-Iran peace deal announced (Image by AI)

item.description: Lower oil prices from the deal could reduce India's energy import bill and current account deficit, easing pressure on the rupee.
item.content:encoded: Lower oil prices from the deal could reduce India's energy import bill and current account deficit, easing pressure on the rupee.

item.title: Australian Girl, 9, Killed In Pakistan After Cop 'Mistakenly' Shoots Her

media:content.media:description: The girl's father told SBS Urdu that cops fired first.

item.description: A cop, thinking that the robbers were fleeing in the family's vehicle, "mistakenly" fired his gun, resulting in the death of the 9-year-old and injuring her brother and father, police said.
item.content:encoded: A cop, thinking that the robbers were fleeing in the family's vehicle, "mistakenly" fired his gun, resulting in the death of the 9-year-old and injuring her brother and father, police said.

item.title: US At Odds With Allies Over How Easy It Is To Reopen Hormuz

media:content.media:description: One senior US official said traffic in the waterway would ramp up over time.

item.description: According to one G7 official, there are serious difficulties in finding a common position among the group about how to deal with the situation in Iran.
item.content:encoded: According to one G7 official, there are serious difficulties in finding a common position among the group about how to deal with the situation in Iran.

item.title: Dhaka Summons Indian Envoy After Bangladesh PM's Adviser Screened At Delhi Airport

media:content.media:description: New Delhi has not commented publicly about the incident.

item.description: The Bangladeshi leader was travelling to India on a government invitation. He reportedly had a regular Bangladeshi passport with a SAARC visa and was not carrying a diplomatic passport.
item.content:encoded: The Bangladeshi leader was travelling to India on a government invitation. He reportedly had a regular Bangladeshi passport with a SAARC visa and was not carrying a diplomatic passport.

item.title: 6.7 Earthquake Shakes Part Of Indonesia, Causes Scattered Damage

media:content.media:description: Indonesia is crossed by several seismic faults, and earthquakes and volcanic activity are common.

item.description: The strong shaking sent people fleeing into open areas in and around Palu, a city of about 400,000 people and the capital of Central Sulawesi province.
item.content:encoded: The strong shaking sent people fleeing into open areas in and around Palu, a city of about 400,000 people and the capital of Central Sulawesi province.

item.title: Palestinian Leader Announces Presidential Election In Early 2027

media:content.media:description: The leader issued a decree introducing changes to electoral procedures.

item.description: Palestinian president Mahmoud Abbas has announced that presidential elections will be held in early 2027 and legislative elections in November this year, without...
item.content:encoded: Palestinian president Mahmoud Abbas has announced that presidential elections will be held in early 2027 and legislative elections in November this year, without...

item.title: Your Smart Doorbell, Tablet May Be Working For Hackers Without You Knowing

media:content.media:description: Hackers and cybercriminals use these infected devices to carry out illegal activities

item.description: The malware, known as residential proxy software, can turn a device into a hidden gateway for internet traffic.
item.content:encoded: The malware, known as residential proxy software, can turn a device into a hidden gateway for internet traffic.

item.title: 'Banaras Connect': PM Modi Visits Varanasi-Inspired Exhibition In Slovakia

media:content.media:description: The exhibition featured paintings created by Slovak artists who had travelled to Varanasi

item.description: The exhibition featured paintings and artworks created by Slovak artists who had recently travelled to Varanasi.
item.content:encoded: The exhibition featured paintings and artworks created by Slovak artists who had recently travelled to Varanasi.

item.title: Attacks On Educational Institutes Surged Over 40% In 2024-2025: Report

media:content.media:description: Report recorded over 1,900 cases of schools and universities being used by military in 2024-2025

item.description: The report, Education Under Attack 2026, recorded at least 8,566 attacks on education during the two-year period, marking an increase of more than 40 per cent compared with 2022 and 2023.
item.content:encoded: The report, Education Under Attack 2026, recorded at least 8,566 attacks on education during the two-year period, marking an increase of more than 40 per cent compared with 2022 and 2023.

item.title: British Police Officer Accused Of Using AI To Fabricate Evidence In Multiple Cases

media:content.media:description: Derbyshire Police probe officer accused of AI use to create false evidential material.

item.description: A British police officer is facing a criminal investigation for allegedly using AI to fabricate evidence.
item.content:encoded: A British police officer is facing a criminal investigation for allegedly using AI to fabricate evidence.

item.title: Utah BASE Jump Kills 2, Including Extreme Athlete Who Performed With Madonna

media:content.media:description: Andy Lewis was a prominent figure in the niche sports of slacklining and tricklining.

item.description: A study focused on BASE jumping in Norway, published in a medical journal in 2007, estimated that BASE jumping carried risks of injury or death five to eight times greater than skydiving.
item.content:encoded: A study focused on BASE jumping in Norway, published in a medical journal in 2007, estimated that BASE jumping carried risks of injury or death five to eight times greater than skydiving.

item.title: India Slams Draft On UN Body Expansion, Calls For Negotiation Clarity

media:content.media:description: India called on the co-chairs to revise the Elements Paper to make it more objective

item.description: India's most concrete demand was a call for the co-chairs to move the process to formal text-based negotiations, with clearly defined milestones and timelines -- consistent with how other UN negotiating processes function.
item.content:encoded: India's most concrete demand was a call for the co-chairs to move the process to formal text-based negotiations, with clearly defined milestones and timelines -- consistent with how other UN negotiating processes function.

item.title: Ukraine Offers Lucrative Fixed-Term Army Contracts To Attract Recruits

media:content.media:description: The new contracts offer lucrative salaries to frontline assault troops.

item.description: Ukraine announced an overhaul of its army recruitment system on Monday, offering lucrative contracts and clearly defined terms of service in a...
item.content:encoded: Ukraine announced an overhaul of its army recruitment system on Monday, offering lucrative contracts and clearly defined terms of service in a...

item.title: 2 Nuke-Delivery Bombers Of Biggest Cold War Enemies Crash On Same Day

media:content.media:description: Only H models of the B-52 remain in the Air Force inventory.

item.description: The B-52 and Tu-22M3 bombers have remained in service for the US and Russian militaries, respectively, for decades, primarily because no direct successors have been developed that fully replicate their combinations of payload, range, flexibility
item.content:encoded: The B-52 and Tu-22M3 bombers have remained in service for the US and Russian militaries, respectively, for decades, primarily because no direct successors have been developed that fully replicate their combinations of payload, range, flexibility

item.title: Elite Skydiver With 6,800 Jumps In 36 Years Among 11 Killed In US Crash

media:content.media:description: Weather did not appear to be a factor behind the crash.

item.description: Kevin Payne, who had jumped with seven of the skydivers on the plane, said they were all different in nearly every way, except that they were all brought together as a "sky family."
item.content:encoded: Kevin Payne, who had jumped with seven of the skydivers on the plane, said they were all different in nearly every way, except that they were all brought together as a "sky family."

item.title: Trump Says Iran 'Agreed' To Never Have Nukes, Rejects $300 Million Fund Claim

media:content.media:description: Vance also backed Trump's position on Iran's nuclear future.

item.description: Team Trump has not yet explained how his agreement will address Iran's nuclear programme, including who will be in charge of verifying that Iran is in compliance and who will destroy or remove highly enriched uranium believed to be buried under nucle
item.content:encoded: <iframe title="Player" class="head-story__img img__full lazyload" height="300" scrolling=no frameborder=no src="about:blank" data-src="https://www.ndtv.com/video/embed-player/id/1113719/play-via-app-wap?pWidth=100&pHeight=100&embed_type=story&site=classic&autostart=1"> </iframe>Team Trump has not yet explained how his agreement will address Iran's nuclear programme, including who will be in charge of verifying that Iran is in compliance and who will destroy or remove highly enriched uranium believed to be buried under nucle

item.title: US Beagle Research Facility That Drew Protests Is Closing, Rescue Group Takes In Dogs

media:content.media:description: Big Dog Ranch Rescue said some of the beagles will go to rescue groups.

item.description: Years-long opposition to the facility came to head in April when a large group of animal welfare activists entered the property in an attempt to take away beagles.
item.content:encoded: Years-long opposition to the facility came to head in April when a large group of animal welfare activists entered the property in an attempt to take away beagles.

item.title: TikTok, Instagram, Snapchat: Apps That Governments Call Dangerous For Kids

media:content.media:description: The coming months will be crucial as governments tighten controls on children's online access.

item.description: Governments across the globe are introducing legislation to ban children from using social media, driven by growing concerns over mental health, cyberbullying, addiction, and exposure to predators online.
item.content:encoded: Governments across the globe are introducing legislation to ban children from using social media, driven by growing concerns over mental health, cyberbullying, addiction, and exposure to predators online.

item.title: "She Was Breathing Heavily": Nurse Recalls Final Moments Of Woman Killed In Bungee Jump Accident

media:content.media:description: Among the first people to reach the victim was off-duty nurse Rayza Dias.

item.description: Off-duty nurse Rayza Dias reportedly rushed to the base of the bridge and stated that Maria was still conscious for a short period of time, briefly talking to her before she died.
item.content:encoded: Off-duty nurse Rayza Dias reportedly rushed to the base of the bridge and stated that Maria was still conscious for a short period of time, briefly talking to her before she died.

item.title: Trump's Iran Accord Offers Exit From War And Fresh Political Risks

media:content.media:description: Now that Trump is negotiating a nuclear deal, he risks opening himself up to Democratic attacks.

item.description: The Trump administration and Iran's leadership agreed on Sunday to terms to end their war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a
item.content:encoded: The Trump administration and Iran's leadership agreed on Sunday to terms to end their war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a
